LAW OFFICES

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

CENTER TOWER
650 TOWN CENTER DRIVE
SUITE 250

COSTA MESA, CA 92626-1981
TELEPHONE (714) 835-9000
FACSIMILE (714) 545-0838

WALTER L. NOSSAMAN
(1886-1964)

LOS ANGELES
THIRTY-FIRST FLOOR
445 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90071-1602
(213) 612-7800

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRTY-FOURTH FLOOR
560 MISSION STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(415) 398-3600

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1227 25TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1156
(202) 223-9100

SACRAMENTO
SUITE 1000
400 S STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-3701
(916) 442-6888

REFER TO FILE NUMBER

August 9, 1990

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Registration Requirements For AB 680 Transportation
Privatization Program Conferences with CalTrans
Representatives on Non Program-Specific Common
Documentation Provisions

Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen:

Our firm is registered under the California Political Reform Act to
represent a number of clients before virtually every Agency and Division of State
government. We also represent the following consortia who have been qualified
by CalTrans to submit proposals for the privatization demonstration projects
under the AB 680 transportation privatization program:

1. "The Perot Group Consortium", comprised of:

The Perot Group
Greiner Engineering Inc.
Amtech Systems Corporation
Wilbur Smith Associates, Inc.
The First Boston Corporation
Putnam, Hayes & Bartlett, Inc.
Traffic Consultants, Inc.
URS/Coverdale & Colpitts
Kiewit Pacific Company
Robert G. Neely
Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
August 9, 1990
Page 2


2.      "PrivaCAL", a consortium comprised of:

             Bechtel Corporation
             D.J. Smith Associates Inc.
             William R. Gray & Company
             Public Affairs Management
             Woodward-Clyde Consultants
             Wilbur Smith Associates
             Kidder, Peabody & Company
             Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe
             Angus McDonald & Associates
             Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott

3.      Bechtel Corporation, a consortium comprised of:

             A.V. Palmer & Associates, Inc.
             KaWES and Associates, Inc.
             Bechtel Corporation
             D.J. Smith Associates Inc.
             William R. Gray & Company
             Public Affairs Management
             Woodward-Clyde Consultants
             Wilbur Smith Associates
             Kidder, Peabody & Company
             Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe
             Angus McDonald & Associates
             Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott

4.      "Transportation Systems Associates", a consortium comprised of:

             T.Y. Lin International
             Morrison-Knudsen Engineers
             Kidder, Peabody & Co., Inc.
             GTM International; France
             Wilbur Smith Associates
             Aaron G. Green and Associates
             Putnam, Hayes & Bartlett
             BRISA, Auto-Estradas de Portugal, S.A.; Portugal
             CGA Camp Transport; France
             Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott

             All qualified consortia were asked by CalTrans to participate in a
series of conferences to begin drafting the common provisions of the franchise
agreements, leases, operation agreements and maintenance agreements to be entered into by CalTrans and the consortia selected by its Director to develop the demonstration projects, unrelated to project-specific issues.

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
August 9, 1990
Page 3


Today, Thursday, August 9, 1990, I talked with you by phone for the
purpose of verifying that participation in this process of CalTrans does not
require our firm to register for these consortia. You confirmed our
understanding that participation in this process does not require registration.
Our analysis is that this CalTrans process would not constitute a
"quasi-legislative administrative action" proceeding for the purposes of
Government Code §82002, requiring registration under §82032, either because it
would not be defined as "... the proposal, drafting, development, consideration,
amendment, enactment or defeat by any State agency of any rule, regulation or
other action in any rate making proceeding or any quasi-legislative proceeding",
in the first instance, or it would be exempt under the provisions of
2 CA Admin.Code 18202 excluding those proceedings "... to which an action is
taken awarding a grant or contract."

While we are confident in our own interpretation of the Act and its
regulations, and are reassured by your phone advice that our interpretation is
correct, we would appreciate your written confirmation of these CalTrans proceedings, if necessary, can be obtained from Carl Williams, Assistant Director of CalTrans, (916) 445-1462 or
Roy Nagy, Deputy Assistant Director of CalTrans, (916) 327-4220.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
James E. Erickson
of NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX
& ELLIOTT

JEE:ag

ERICKSON:1206/5
